Bladesman's Sword  
 

Lore Item Placeable
Slot: PRIMARY SECONDARY
Skill: 1H Slashing Atk Delay: 36
DMG: 12 Dmg Bonus: 35
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
WT: 2.0 Size: MEDIUM
Class: WAR PAL RNG SHD BRD ROG
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

# Jan 25 2004 at 12:45 PM Rating: Decent

This has been renamed to the Bladesman Sword. Probably due to the upcoming GoD.

Ehhh so so
QuoteReply
# Dec 21 2002 at 11:33 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592256336865
6 posts
This weapon is alright. I got it for 40pp at FV from this desperate guy. It was alright I guess. But then I went to the Bazaar and got an IcePaw War Claw, and it's even better! So If you're looking for a cheap weapon with nothing but ATTACK. Then the Berserker sword is good.

my 30th lvl bard still has this 1..lol

hummm
QuoteReply
# Sep 11 2002 at 10:39 PM Rating: Default
Valdon
674 posts
Im a little upset at my girlfriend. we akk know weapons get better and better. and lately some CRAZY good weapons are available through vendors for screwed up prices. I gave my girl THIS sword a long time ago to play with and hold for me. and I recently bought some shaded sword, like a 12/24 (much nicer) from a vendor for like 6pp !!!! and well, i ask about THIS blade reminding me of this nice shaded one and she gave it away.

my point?

the new screwed up economy, alows me to buy a better weapon for 6pp from a vendor. and also not to let my girl use my damn swords. this USED to be a rather nice blade.
____________________________
· 'Valdon Gravedancer' Terris-Thule. Dwarven Cleric 34th circle.
· 'Junglebug Sugarpants' Terris-Thule. Halfling Druid. 52nd season.
· 'Shinook Eldergod' Terris-Thule. Troll Shaman of 32nd rite.
· 'Soultree Vixen' Terris-Thule. Woodelf Ranger of 34th season.
▲ Page top
Not all that good
QuoteReply
# Sep 02 2002 at 3:31 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592251536253
24 posts
This sword isnt that good I mean u can get like a Sporali Defenders Blade or something for around the same price and it does much more damage with just a tenth of a second added on (17 37)
▲ Page top
must ask..
QuoteReply
# Jun 03 2002 at 10:55 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591779489507
1 post
What is the overall best weapon combination for a Warrior ??
▲ Page top
RE: must ask..
QuoteReply
# Jul 23 2002 at 1:52 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592254998940
23 posts
I would just like to say there isn't. A warrior is good with everything therefore just go with like the best ratio weapons and not the best damage or delay weapons. People think o it's damage is really low so this is a great weapon true that it does make u hit more often whicch can make the target hit less BUT if the damage is 1 and delay is 19 the weapon would suck. On the other hand people think that damage is needed more than the delay such as the weighted axe which is 45 150 i believe well it hards but u get hit more often and is a big hassle at higher lvls especially when the guys start hitting for 150+.

its ok
QuoteReply
# Mar 04 2002 at 7:43 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592252285552
22 posts
This sword is ok, I put it in the offhand slot and wurmslayer in primary. I know, I know very very SLOW Warrior, but this is what I use if no one else in group dishes out good damage and I can get a haste type spell cast on me. If I am MT I may use ssoy and this in offhand or velium battle axe in offhand so as to speed up the hitting and hopefully hold taunt. Anyway, gonna keep this weapon till i get a centi ls or a Lammy. Take care


Thorno Ironfaith <True Valor>
Warrior of the 39th season
▲ Page top
Hand-me-down
QuoteReply
# Feb 11 2002 at 3:05 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592758968592
11 posts
Another higher level warrior gave me this item out of the blue (no-charge) when I was fighting with him at orc camp 2. I was level 8 at the time. I'm now level 12. I didn't know what it was until I weilded it. Then my damage/hit went from an average 12 to a consistant 25. Very cool indeed! Its the only item I have that's "Lore-like"; all my other items were earned through sweat and hard work (and dying alot!)
▲ Page top
offhand or not offhand
QuoteReply
# Nov 19 2001 at 10:18 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591761425753
20 posts
i've read a couple of messages posted by many higher lvl players saying that they keep this weapong off hand in favor of seemingly faster delay weapons, i personaly am having trouble keeping agro and i am using this weapon in my primary hand and my weaker velium battle ax in my secondary hand, the axe has about 8 damage and 28 delay, which should i keep in my primary hand, i am a lvl 16 twink, and i am in need of advice.

Krisaack 16 and a third warrior.
▲ Page top
RE: offhand or not offhand
QuoteReply
# Jan 23 2002 at 6:05 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1593361843122
Scholar
61 posts
Anyway here is some advice instead of flames =P

Taunt, as I understand it, is governed by (for this purpose, I know there is much more to aggro...) two things: a) damagae dealt and b)number of touches (hits and i think misses still draw some aggro)

So...if you put the faster weapon in your primary you will get more touches but cause less damage. But if it is in your secondary you will get way less touches because you also have to successfully roll for duelwield to even swing.

I bet if you were to put the faster weapon in your primary you would draw more aggro but do a little less damage...try it out and see whats best. I have a 16 warr too and picked up a Glimmering Axe for 50pp (6/19 1hs) and I swing like crazy in my primary and have not trouble drawing aggro.

Hope this helps, good luck to ya :)

16 Oge War
25 Druid of Tunare
▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Jan 10 2002 at 1:46 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) No offense but if you are using this and a vel axe you are not a twink. I think your character is an Alt which is simply a second or 3rd character of a player. A twink is a low level character that uses expensive high level gear given to him usually by the players own high level character. Just semantics really. Anyway have fun.
RE: offhand or not offhand
QuoteReply
# Apr 05 2002 at 5:47 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591765923498
Guru
170 posts
Maybe I'm being old-school or I'm craving for some stuff with filling but last time I checked it's our alternate characters with better gear that are called twinks. Items handed down or to or whatever by another player we sometimes call tweaking.
▲ Page top
RE: offhand or not offhand
QuoteReply
# Jan 14 2002 at 3:59 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757411902
106 posts
I can't believe you just wasted my brain cells by defining what a twink is.

In my mind if you are using ANY equipment you didn't pay for with money you raised personally or loot yourself and it came from a MOB you could not defeat without assistance of a much higher level player then you are a twink. A level 5 in full crafted is twinked, I don't care what you say.

Now there...I've wasted your time as well.
▲ Page top
RE: offhand or not offhand
QuoteReply
# Jan 22 2002 at 6:58 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361915516
29 posts
The only time wasted here is when u decide to conjure stupid replies to other replies.

I got a berserker sword handed down from high lvl char when i was around lvl 6 or 7 because he wanted to help out a fellow ranger, not becuase he wanted to cheat the system. Being lvl 12 now i see that it was one of the best things that happened to me. Even in roleplaying i think that its okay because would not that happen in real life? One character has extra hard-earned equipment and doesn't want to sell it and waste its value, so he gives it to another in the same class as a gift. Thats how u make friends, buddy.

Its okay if its not overdone. I believe that there is a limit that each player should set for his own character. Lvl 5 in full-crafted is a bit much, i agree.

Lestolian 12 Woody Ranger
Tarew Marr
▲ Page top
RE: offhand or not offhand
QuoteReply
# Oct 19 2003 at 10:54 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592259716847
5 posts
I agree i was wandering aimlessly through G-Fay wondering if i should even keep playing EQ i was lost had junk for equipment and had no idea what i was doing at lvl 7.... then i just sat down near the BB/G-Fay zone line and was thinking and some 40th+ lvl person asked me why i was sitting there i said i dont know what to do i have junk, and i keep dieing so he gave me a BABS well now that seems like junk to me but at the time i was like wow this is the best sword i ever saw. it gave me hope as a newbie...


My point?

giving now and then is deffinitly a good thing.
it sometimes comes back to help you later...




Gremag the Grey 45th lvl Paladin Druzzil Ro
▲ Page top
RE: offhand or not offhand

# Feb 22 2002 at 10:19 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591770977785
15 posts
I tend to agree. A twink is someone who goes out of the way to farm stuff for a low lvl alt. If I am camping orcs in High Pass for a Shralok pack, and I get the Shiny Brass Halberd instead. I'd give the SBH to a low level cause it is a nice thing to do, and it isn't worth the time to go sell it.
▲ Page top
RE: offhand or not offhand
QuoteReply
# Feb 05 2002 at 9:15 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1593361934028
4 posts
Yeah only one or two items perhaps, but if he's got great stuff and in a bunch of slots, I'll call him a twink
▲ Page top
RE: offhand or not offhand
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2003 at 9:49 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966342790
View User Forum
85 posts
Well, i am a mechanic (plese see explaination below). This was given to me by a 30 + ranger, so i am grateful. What I have been using, however, is a shiv, 5/22. I hit very fast, and my piercing and offence is almost always maxed by the next lvl. =) NOw, I have been using this only as a backup, such as when I may want to fight a magic creature, lets just say a Willo'wisp. I am lvl 5, so I won't be fighting wisps for a while, but still, it pays to be prepared. As a question, when I hit 12 and can dual, I was going to put another shiv in my off-hand because of its speed. Would tis be a correct assumption? Thanks you for you CONSTRUCTIVE criticism.


Montrosse.

Level 5 mechanic Luclin Server.

"Please, don't call me a spy, or a rogue, or an assassin, or a thief. I am a mechanic. When you need something, or someone, fixed or "Taken care of", you call a mechanic.

It does not proc
QuoteReply
# Sep 10 2001 at 5:30 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591766104124
4 posts
It does not proc you always go Bezerk when you go belo 20% hp you smart guys :P
▲ Page top
RE: It does not proc
QuoteReply
# Nov 14 2001 at 4:40 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592250760013
View User Forum
49 posts
I have never heard anyone I met in game who has used this sword say anything about it proccing "Berserk." Honestly, I don't believe it does. Aside from that, I'd like to bring up the Warrior's Berserk threshold. Everyone seems to be saying that a Warrior will go Berserk when his/her health drops below 20%. Since I read that, I have been paying closer attention. I have noted that my Warrior will go Berserk when his health drops to 30%, and, when healing after the fight, will sometimes remain Berserk until almost 40% health. Is 20% the actual threshold for the Berserk ability?
▲ Page top
RE: It does not proc
QuoteReply
# Sep 30 2001 at 1:40 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591757619301
20 posts
Just to add some information:

Does not "proc" in any fashion for me--been using one offhand with my ibc since about 35. Am 43 now, still not proccing (if it did "proc" berserk, i'd notice a str increase just like when I do go berzerk...)

Does seem to do quite alot of damage on criticals and cripplings, though, for an offhand weapon.

Xarthaz
43 Iksar Warrior
E'ci
▲ Page top
RE: It does not proc
QuoteReply
# Sep 12 2001 at 1:56 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591764914131
16 posts
If you are refering to DamonBartn's post...

You did not read it correctly. He was explaining the warrior's ability to go berserk when under 20% hp, and with this sword it procs the same ability regardless of how much health you have.

I still would like to get my hands on one to see if it actually procs. Until then, I debate the statement that it does. You would think Verant would list something like that in the item descript.

Yurgonn,
30th level Warrior,
Solusek Ro.

proc
QuoteReply
# Jul 27 2001 at 8:12 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592248480925
3 posts
I just bought one of these a few days ago. I am a 40 Warrior, and it procs "Berserk". When a warrior goes below 20% of hit points he will go Berserk and have more chances of critical hits. A warrior can go Berserk with this sword without going below 20% of hit points. It procs for me non stop, but am not sure at what level it will start to proc.
